rulings.Case Title: CONNAN KING ET AL VS. GREGORY JONES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: REAL PROPERTY/ HOUSING COURT LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR JULY 6, 2020 LINE 2 DEFENDANT GREGORY JONES MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. DENIED. Triable issue of fact exists as to the date of accrual of the cause of action and Plaintiff's diligence in finding/confirming that a cause of action exists. Contrary to the Defendant's assertion, unlike in Manefee, this complaint is not based on a facially defective Notice. Nothing in the Complaint suggests that the Notice was in any way defectively drafted or served. Tenant's diligence in confirming Landlord's alleged subjective wrongful intent and subsequent violation (e.g. failure to timely move-in or to stay for requisite period of time) is an issue of fact to be determined by the jury. Prevailing party to prepare an order in compliance with CCP 437c(g). = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SAN DONG BEST INC. ET AL VS. GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC. ET AL

rulings.Case Title: SAN DONG BEST INC. ET AL VS. GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: REAL PROPERTY/ HOUSING COURT LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR JULY 6, 2020 LINE 3 DEFENDANT DAVID TSAO's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT READ THIS NOTIFICATION IN ITS ENTIRETY. Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Defendant's demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege what action/inaction by the Defendant constituted breach as to the term requiring that the sale be completed within 3 months. It does not appear from the operative complaint that Plaintiff alleges a breach of any other term of the alleged contract. Defendant's demurrer to the third cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action under Cal. Civ. Code § 1950.8.=(501/CFH)
